prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training with high-intensity endurance performance

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all meals for one day (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks)
All foods are vegan and gluten-free
Plan provides adequate calories and protein for marathon training recovery
Meals are concise and practical to prepare
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for an athlete
scenario: Testing with a high-performance endurance athlete (marathon runner) with multiple dietary restrictions (vegan, gluten-free) to evaluate meal plan feasibility while maintaining caloric and nutrient density requirements

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains at least 4 meals/snacks distributed throughout the day
Total daily protein intake is at least 2.6g per kg of body weight (338g minimum)
Total daily caloric intake supports muscle building for a 130kg athlete (approximately 3500-4000 calories)
Meal plan is presented in a compact, concise format suitable for quick reference
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for an athlete
scenario: Testing with a strength-training athlete (weightlifter) with extreme body metrics (very tall and heavy) to assess whether the meal plan appropriately scales protein intake and total calories for muscle building goals

prompt_inputs.goal: Maintain lean muscle while reducing body fat for competition

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains exactly 4 meals (breakfast, lunch, snack, dinner) with portion sizes appropriate for a 52 kg athlete
Total daily calories create a moderate deficit (approximately 1,800-2,000 kcal) suitable for weight management
Includes adequate protein (minimum 1.2g per kg body weight) to preserve muscle during deficit
Plan is presented in compact, easy-to-read format with clear meal timing
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for an athlete
scenario: Testing with a lightweight sport athlete (gymnast) with minimal restrictions but weight management goals to verify the meal plan balances nutritional completeness with controlled portion sizes and caloric deficits
